data.title: New White Paper Calls for Precision Approach to Section 232 in Medical Device Sector
data.description: On April 14, 2026, Antonio Ortiz-Mena, the Chief Executive Officer of AOM Advisors, and Christopher G. Sheeron, the Chief Executive Officer of Action for Health, unveiled a white paper titled "Critical Care: Safeguarding U.S. Medical Device Superiority via Targeted Section 232 Action." This document delves into strategies to preserve America's position in medical device innovation.

data.title: T1 Energy Announces Pricing of Upsized Public Offering of Convertible Senior Notes Due 2031
data.description: T1 Energy Inc., a company traded on the NYSE under the symbol TE, disclosed on April 14, 2026, that they have priced their public offering of $160.0 million aggregate principal Convertible Notes due 2031. This amount signifies an increase from the initially indicated $125.0 million. The Convertible Notes carry a 4.00% interest rate.

data.title: Youlife Group Inc. Announces Full Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call
data.description: On April 14, 2026, in Shanghai, Youlife Group Inc. (NASDAQ: YOUL), a prominent provider of blue-collar lifetime services in China, disclosed its plans for an earnings conference call. The call aims to discuss the Company's essential operational and financial outcomes.

data.title: TeraWulf Announces Pricing of Upsized Common Stock Offering
data.description: On April 14, 2026, TeraWulf Inc. disclosed the pricing details of its public common stock offering. The Company, listed as WULF on NASDAQ, set the price at $19.00 per share for 47,400,000 shares. This pricing resulted in gross proceeds amounting to around $900 million, an increase from the initial target of $800 million. The Offering is slated to be finalized on April 16, 2026, pending the completion of standard closing requirements.

data.title: Interfor to Announce First Quarter Results on May 14, 2026
data.description: Investors and analysts are invited to join the conference call scheduled for 8:00 a.m. Pacific Time on Friday, May 15, 2026. This call will provide important insights and updates for stakeholders.

data.title: MNA: Baystate Franklin Nurses Vote 98.2% to Authorize a Potential Strike After Filing Unfair Labor Practice Charge to Protect Patient Care
data.description: Nurses at Baystate Franklin Medical Center in GREENFIELD, Mass. made a significant statement by voting to oppose an illegal staffing proposal and demanding a fair contract that prioritizes patient safety and local care. The registered nurses at BFMC are seeking to protect these crucial aspects by sending a strong message through their vote. This development occurred on April 14, 2026, as highlighted by PRNewswire.
